steps.title: Use Desmos to make the table
steps.content: The fastest check is to read two clean points from the line, such as $(0,-5)$ and $(1,-3)$, then fit the line in Desmos. Enter $x_1=[0,1]$, $y_1=[-5,-3]$, and $y_1\sim mx_1+b$. Desmos gives $m=2$ and $b=-5$, so the graph is $y=2x-5$.

steps.title: Test x-values in each choice
steps.content: Now use the line to generate the three requested table entries: when $x=0$, $y=-5$; when $x=1$, $y=-3$; when $x=2$, $y=-1$. That gives the table $(0,-5)$, $(1,-3)$, $(2,-1)$, which matches only choice D. D is correct.
